Title: Memory Manager — MEMORY.md Load, Update, and Limit Enforcement [1311482a-07c8-4ce8-824c-2b3162980fba]
Description: As a developer, I want a memory-manager module that loads MEMORY.md at session start and enforces a 2200-character hard limit so that COSA's persistent memory never grows unbounded.
Acceptance Criteria:
  1. loadMemory() reads data/MEMORY.md and returns its contents; returns an empty template if the file does not exist
  2. updateMemory(patch) merges patch fields into the existing MEMORY.md sections and writes back to disk
  3. writeMemory(content) replaces MEMORY.md entirely after enforcing the character limit
  4. If a write would exceed 2200 characters, the oldest Recent Incidents entry is pruned and the write is retried
  5. If still over limit after pruning incidents, the Notes section is truncated to 100 characters
  6. The 2200-character hard limit is never exceeded after any write
  7. updateMemory() implemented as a SAM acceptor per Phase 2 §19.4
--- END STORY ---

Title: Context Compressor — Summarize Middle Turns [fe1d2400-87df-46be-8b59-dd3b8244318f]
Description: As a developer, I want a context-compressor module that detects when the message array is too long and summarizes the middle turns via Haiku so that long sessions never exceed context limits.
Acceptance Criteria:
  1. needsCompression(messages) returns true when messages.length exceeds config.contextCompression.maxTurnsBeforeCompress
  2. compress(messages, sessionId) protects the first 3 and last 4 turns
  3. compress() calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to summarize the middle turns
  4. The summary is injected as a single user message prefixed with '[Context summary — prior turns compressed]'
  5. The returned array length equals protectFirstN + 1 + protectLastN = 8 (with default config)
  6. A compression event is logged to session.db as a system-role turn
  7. Compression is implemented as a SAM action/acceptor pair per Phase 2 §19.3
  8. Acceptor rejects (no Haiku call) if needsCompression returns false
--- END STORY ---

089908dd-9b60-457b-b199-ad218e666b67.markdown:
## Context
- Branch: phase-2
- Dependencies: none (this is the foundational FSM story)
- Relevant existing files: src/orchestrator.js (consumes FSM events), src/approval-engine.js (may gate FSM transitions), package.json (verify @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm is listed), docs/cosa_phase2_spec.md (phase 2 requirements)
- skills.db is the SQLite persistence target; check if a db adapter already exists under src/ before creating one

## Objective
This story implements SkillCreationFSM, a finite state machine that governs the full lifecycle of skill creation — from novelty evaluation through FTS search, AI generation, schema validation, and database persistence. By wrapping every step in a @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm machine, the implementation ensures each transition is explicit, retry logic is bounded, and no skill is saved until it passes validation, satisfying all 7 acceptance criteria in story 089908dd-9b60-457b-b199-ad218e666b67.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. SkillCreationFSM has all 6 states: idle, evaluating, searching, generating, validating, persisted
2. not_novel event from evaluating transitions to idle without calling skills_fts
3. match_found event from searching transitions to idle (existing skill flagged for improvement)
4. no_match event from searching transitions to generating
5. invalid event from validating transitions back to generating for up to 2 retries
6. retry_exceeded after 2 failed validations transitions to idle and logs failure without saving a skill
7. Happy path idle → persisted results in a new row in skills.db

## Codebase Navigation
Before writing any code, run the following Code Model queries to understand the existing landscape:

- Run hdsl_search pattern='sam-fsm' to find any prior FSM instantiation or usage patterns in the codebase
- Run hdsl_search pattern='skills' to locate any existing skills database adapter, skills FTS module, or skills schema definitions
- Run hdsl_peek path='src/orchestrator.js' to understand how the orchestrator currently triggers skill-related workflows and where SkillCreationFSM should be wired in
- Run hdsl_peek path='src/approval-engine.js' to check whether approval gates are expected to be integrated with FSM transitions
- Run hdsl_search pattern='better-sqlite3 OR Database OR skills.db' to find any existing SQLite setup
- Run hdsl_deps path='src/orchestrator.js' before modifying it to understand all dependents
- Run hdsl_search pattern='createMachine OR defineMachine OR FSM OR StateMachine' to learn the @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm API shape already used elsewhere
- Check package.json to confirm @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm version and any peer dependencies

## Implementation Instructions

### 1. File: src/skill-creation-fsm.js

Purpose: Defines and exports the SkillCreationFSM class. Encapsulates the @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm machine config, service invocations, retry counter, and all transition guards. This is the primary deliverable for this story.

Exports:
- default export: SkillCreationFSM (class)
- named export: SKILL_CREATION_STATES (frozen object of state name constants)
- named export: SKILL_CREATION_EVENTS (frozen object of event name constants)

State constants (SKILL_CREATION_STATES):
  IDLE: 'idle'
  EVALUATING: 'evaluating'
  SEARCHING: 'searching'
  GENERATING: 'generating'
  VALIDATING: 'validating'
  PERSISTED: 'persisted'

Event constants (SKILL_CREATION_EVENTS):
  START: 'start'
  IS_NOVEL: 'is_novel'
  NOT_NOVEL: 'not_novel'
  MATCH_FOUND: 'match_found'
  NO_MATCH: 'no_match'
  GENERATED: 'generated'
  VALID: 'valid'
  INVALID: 'invalid'
  RETRY_EXCEEDED: 'retry_exceeded'

Class: SkillCreationFSM

Constructor: constructor({ skillsDb, skillsFts, generator, validator, logger })
  - skillsDb: object with method insertSkill(skill: object): Promise<number> returning the new row id
  - skillsFts: object with method search(query: string): Promise<Array<object>> returning matching skill records
  - generator: object with method generate(incident: object): Promise<object> returning a raw skill draft
  - validator: object with method validate(skillDraft: object): { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }
  - logger: object with methods info(msg, meta), warn(msg, meta), error(msg, meta) — defaults to console if not provided
  - All dependencies are required; throw TypeError('Missing dependency: <name>') if any is falsy

Internal state shape stored in this._context:
  {
    state: string,          // current state name from SKILL_CREATION_STATES
    retryCount: number,     // counts invalid events from validating; resets on each new START
    incident: object|null,  // the incident payload passed to start()
    skillDraft: object|null,// the generated skill candidate
    matchedSkill: object|null, // populated when match_found
    persistedId: number|null,  // row id after persisting
    error: Error|null       // last error if any
  }

Public methods:

start(incident: object): Promise<void>
  - Asserts current state is IDLE; throws Error('FSM not idle') if not
  - Resets context: sets incident, clears retryCount to 0, clears skillDraft/matchedSkill/persistedId/error
  - Transitions state to EVALUATING
  - Calls this._runEvaluation(incident)

getState(): string
  - Returns this._context.state

getContext(): object
  - Returns a shallow copy of this._context (do not expose the live reference)

Private methods:

async _runEvaluation(incident: object): Promise<void>
  - Calls this._evaluateNovelty(incident)
  - If result.novel === true: transition to SEARCHING, then call this._runSearch(incident)
  - If result.novel === false: emit NOT_NOVEL, transition to IDLE, return (do NOT call skillsFts.search)
  - Wrap in try/catch; on error set context.error and transition to IDLE with a logged error

async _evaluateNovelty(incident: object): Promise<{ novel: boolean }>
  - Implementation placeholder: subclasses or injected evaluator should override; default implementation returns { novel: true } so tests can inject behavior via the generator or a stub
  - Actually: accept an optional evaluator dep in constructor ({ evaluator }) with method evaluate(incident): Promise<{ novel: boolean }>; if not provided, default to always returning { novel: true }
  - Add evaluator to required dep checks with a fallback default

async _runSearch(incident: object): Promise<void>
  - Calls this.skillsFts.search(incident.description || incident.query || JSON.stringify(incident))
  - If results.length > 0: set context.matchedSkill = results[0], log info('existing skill found for improvement', { skillId: results[0].id }), transition to IDLE with MATCH_FOUND event
  - If results.length === 0: transition to GENERATING with NO_MATCH event, call this._runGeneration(incident)
  - Wrap in try/catch; on error set context.error, log error, transition to IDLE

async _runGeneration(incident: object): Promise<void>
  - Calls this.generator.generate(incident)
  - Sets context.skillDraft = result
  - Transitions to VALIDATING with GENERATED event
  - Calls this._runValidation()
  - Wrap in try/catch; on error set context.error, log error, transition to IDLE

async _runValidation(): Promise<void>
  - Calls this.validator.validate(context.skillDraft)
  - If result.valid === true: call this._runPersist()
  - If result.valid === false:
    - Increment context.retryCount
    - If context.retryCount < 2: log warn('skill validation failed, retrying', { retryCount }), transition to GENERATING with INVALID event, call this._runGeneration(context.incident)
    - If context.retryCount >= 2: log error('skill validation failed after max retries', { retryCount, errors: result.errors }), emit RETRY_EXCEEDED, transition to IDLE, do NOT call _runPersist
  - Wrap in try/catch; on error set context.error, log error, transition to IDLE

async _runPersist(): Promise<void>
  - Calls this.skillsDb.insertSkill(context.skillDraft)
  - Sets context.persistedId = returned id
  - Transitions to PERSISTED with VALID event
  - Logs info('skill persisted', { id: context.persistedId })
  - Wrap in try/catch; on error set context.error, log error, transition to IDLE

_transition(newState: string, eventName: string): void
  - Sets context.state = newState
  - Logs info('FSM transition', { from: previousState, to: newState, event: eventName })

Integration with @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm:
  - Run hdsl_search pattern='sam-fsm' first to learn the exact import and API
  - If sam-fsm exports a createMachine(config) factory, define the machine config as a pure data object and call createMachine with it; store the machine instance in this._machine
  - The machine config must enumerate all 6 states as keys under a 'states' property
  - All transitions described above must be reflected in the machine config's 'on' handlers for each state
  - If sam-fsm does not handle async side-effects, keep async logic in the private _run* methods and use the machine only for state tracking; call this._machine.send(event) before and after async operations as appropriate
  - If sam-fsm uses an interpret/service pattern (like XState), wrap the machine in a service and subscribe to state changes
  - Do not duplicate transition logic between the machine config and the _run* methods — the machine is the source of truth for valid transitions; _run* methods trigger events on the machine

### 2. File: src/skills-db.js (create only if hdsl_search confirms it does not already exist)

Purpose: Thin SQLite adapter over skills.db using better-sqlite3. Provides insertSkill and any future read methods.

Exports:
- default export: SkillsDb class

Constructor: constructor(dbPath: string)
  - Opens or creates the SQLite database at dbPath using better-sqlite3
  - Runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skills (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, body TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')))

Public methods:

insertSkill(skill: { name: string, description: string, body: string }): number
  - Runs INSERT INTO skills (name, description, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?)
  - Returns lastInsertRowid as number
  - Throws if skill is missing required fields (name)

close(): void
  - Calls db.close()

### 3. File: tests/skill-creation-fsm.test.js

Purpose: Unit tests verifying all 7 acceptance criteria. Use Jest (or the test framework found in package.json).

Test structure — describe('SkillCreationFSM', () => { ... }):

Before each test, create stub dependencies:
  - mockSkillsDb: { insertSkill: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(42) }
  - mockSkillsFts: { search: jest.fn() }
  - mockGenerator: { generate: jest.fn() }
  - mockValidator: { validate: jest.fn() }
  - mockEvaluator: { evaluate: jest.fn() }
  - mockLogger: { info: jest.fn(), warn: jest.fn(), error: jest.fn() }

Test cases:

'has all 6 states defined in SKILL_CREATION_STATES':
  - Import SKILL_CREATION_STATES; assert it contains idle, evaluating, searching, generating, validating, persisted

'not_novel event from evaluating transitions to idle without calling skills_fts':
  - mockEvaluator.evaluate.mockResolvedValue({ novel: false })
  - Call fsm.start(incident); await resolution
  - Assert fsm.getState() === 'idle'
  - Assert mockSkillsFts.search was NOT called

'match_found from searching transitions to idle and flags existing skill':
  - mockEvaluator.evaluate.mockResolvedValue({ novel: true })
  - mockSkillsFts.search.mockResolvedValue([{ id: 7, name: 'existing' }])
  - Call fsm.start(incident); await
  - Assert fsm.getState() === 'idle'
  - Assert fsm.getContext().matchedSkill.id === 7
  - Assert mockGenerator.generate was NOT called

'no_match from searching transitions to generating':
  - mockEvaluator.evaluate.mockResolvedValue({ novel: true })
  - mockSkillsFts.search.mockResolvedValue([])
  - mockGenerator.generate.mockResolvedValue({ name: 'new', description: 'd', body: 'b' })
  - mockValidator.validate.mockReturnValue({ valid: true })

Acceptance Criteria:
  1. Subject line is '[COSA] Weekly Digest: week of YYYY-MM-DD'
  2. Email body matches the format in §11.2: health check summary, backup summary, anomalies, skills, operator activity
  3. Digest gathers data via session_search calls for backup and health events
  4. Skills section reports new skills created and skills improved this week
  5. No second digest is sent if a digest was sent within the past 6 days
  6. Email is plain text — no HTML
--- END STORY ---

  8. T-2.8: Session search — COSA calls session_search and returns excerpts from matching prior sessions
  9. T-2.9: Weekly digest — email sent with correct subject; no second digest within 6 days
  10. T-2.10: CLI interface — health_check called and response printed; process exits cleanly on 'exit'
